facets.evidence: `README.md` names purpose, quickstart, scripts, docs, requirements, and operational surfaces. Public voice repair remains required.
`.flywheel/MISSION.md`, `.flywheel/GOAL.md`, `.flywheel/STATE.md`, `PLAN-INFRA-V5.md`, and `PLAN-INFRA-V5.1.md` expose repo lineage, though mission copy is still placeholder.

`.flywheel/scripts/publishability-bar.sh --doctor --json --repo /Users/josh/Developer/zeststream-infra` emits the publishability doctor signal; lifecycle scripts expose health, audit, and runbook paths.

`scripts/self-test.sh`, `scripts/phase-gate.sh`, `scripts/lifecycle-audit.sh`, `scripts/lifecycle-audit-json.sh`, and `scripts/brand-audit.sh` provide validation surfaces. Existing self-test failures are tracked as repo health, not this voice audit.
`scripts/install.sh` exists, but this audit did not find a complete uninstall/reconcile proof for symlinks, registries, and external service state.
Shell and Python surfaces are named by lifecycle responsibility, docs/contracts describe interfaces, and state/templates/registry separation is legible.
`scripts/project-inception.sh`, `scripts/self-test.sh`, `scripts/fleet-health-dashboard.sh`, and `docs/runbook.md` give a first-look operator path without oral handoff.
